instructions:
Upgrade `.docs-ops/extractors/typescript-extractor.py` from v1.1 to v1.2 to capture types declared inside `declare global { namespace Amity { ... } }` blocks in the SDK's `@types/` directory.

CONTEXT: The TS SDK has a pattern where many types (e.g., `AmityClient`, `AmityComment`, `AmityCommentReferenceType`, `InvitationTargetType`, `LiveCollection`, `StreamStatus`) are NOT exported through the barrel (`packages/sdk/src/index.ts`) ‚Äî they're declared as members of a global `Amity` namespace via TypeScript module augmentation in files under `packages/sdk/src/@types/`. These types ARE part of the public API but are referenced as `Amity.XxxType` rather than imported by name.

The triage report (`.docs-ops/evals/ts-accuracy-drift-triage.json`) flagged 1 EXTRACTOR_BLIND_SPOT and several `import Amity*` items in GENUINE_DRIFT and DOC_FABRICATION that are likely false positives caused by this gap. After this upgrade, those should resolve naturally.

WHAT TO ADD: a parser pass that walks every `.ts` file under `AmityTypescriptSDK/packages/sdk/src/@types/` and:

1. Finds `declare global { namespace Amity { ... } }` blocks (also accept `declare global { namespace Amity {`, `declare global { \n namespace Amity {`, and other whitespace/newline variants ‚Äî common Prettier outputs).
2. Within each Amity-namespace block, captures top-level declarations. Use a brace-depth tracker rooted at the opening `{` of the `namespace Amity` block so nested blocks (interface bodies, function-type braces) don't terminate it early.
3. Captures these declaration kinds inside the namespace body:
   - `type <Name> = ...` ‚Üí kind=`type`
   - `interface <Name> ...` ‚Üí kind=`interface`
   - `const <Name> ...` or `const enum <Name> ...` ‚Üí kind=`const` or `enum`
   - `enum <Name> ...` ‚Üí kind=`enum`
   - `function <Name>(...)` ‚Üí kind=`function`
   - `class <Name> ...` ‚Üí kind=`class`
   - Note: declarations inside `declare global { namespace Amity { ... } }` do NOT need an `export` keyword to be part of the namespace's public surface ‚Äî they are public BY VIRTUE of being inside it.
4. Adds a synthetic top-level namespace entry `Amity` to the surface JSON, parallel to the existing 24 namespaces. Members are the declarations captured. Include `source_module` (use the @types subdirectory or a list of contributing files), `member_count`, `members[]`, and an empty `sub_namespaces` (Amity may have nested namespaces but v1.2 can flatten ‚Äî note this in unhandled[] if encountered).

MERGING ACROSS FILES: multiple @types files may each contribute declarations to `namespace Amity`. The extractor must AGGREGATE ‚Äî collect declarations from all files into a single `Amity` namespace entry, deduping by name (if a name appears in multiple files, keep both as separate members ‚Äî they're augmenting the same type ‚Äî and note this in `notes` of the result).

BUMP `extractor_version` to `0.1.2`.

Do NOT change existing namespace/root-export logic. Add the `@types/` Amity-namespace pass as a new step that runs after the barrel walk and writes its result into `surface['namespaces']['Amity']`.

UPDATE `stats`: add a separate stat `amity_global_members` so it's distinguishable. The existing `namespaced_members_total` should include them (sum of all namespace member_counts).

After the extractor upgrade, RE-RUN the validator and check:
  - References like `Amity.LiveCollection`, `Amity.InvitationTargetType`, `Amity.StreamStatus` now validate as known.
  - The 4√ó `import AmityClient`, 6√ó `import AmityComment`, 4√ó `import AmityCommentReferenceType` drift items either disappear or change shape (they were flagged because the bare-name import isn't valid ‚Äî the correct ref is `Amity.AmityClient` etc.). The DRIFT they represent (wrong import syntax) is now MORE actionable, not less: the validator should now show that the type IS in `Amity.*` so a rename task can fix the import.

Report in `notes`: number of @types files scanned, total members added to `Amity` namespace, new total namespaced_members count vs the previous 378, and how the drift report changed (before/after total issue counts).

validators.name: amity_namespace_present_with_members
validators.command: python3 .docs-ops/extractors/typescript-extractor.py > /dev/null && python3 -c "import json; s=json.load(open('.docs-ops/sdk-surface/typescript.json')); assert 'Amity' in s['namespaces'], 'Amity namespace not in surface'; assert s['namespaces']['Amity']['member_count'] > 10, f'unexpectedly few Amity members: {s[\"namespaces\"][\"Amity\"][\"member_count\"]}'; assert s['extractor_version']=='0.1.2', f'version not bumped: {s[\"extractor_version\"]}'; print('OK')"
